newsSince: Material: On June 15, 2026 IQE and Tower Semiconductor announced a multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement for silicon-photonics platforms serving AI-driven data-center infrastructure (200Gb/lane pluggables, 400Gb/lane modulator prototyping, optical-circuit-switches), with minimum purchase/supply commitments. The deal also settles the prior IP dispute, granting IQE a royalty-free license for certain porous-silicon patents.

sources.label: IQE-Tower multi-year InP epiwafer supply agreement (GlobeNewswire)

verdicts.rankRationale: Test-time-scaling lens rewards upstream epitaxy bottleneck exposure. The June 15 Tower InP deal turns IQE's AI-datacenter-optics thesis from latent to contracted (minimum commitments) and clears the IP overhang. A genuine fundamental upgrade for a name sitting near the bottom of this page; move up.
verdicts.bull: Multi-year InP epiwafer commitments tied to AI-datacenter optics (200/400Gb pluggables, OCS); IP dispute settled; scarce scaled epitaxy supply.
verdicts.neutral: Volumes/revenue magnitude not quantified; epitaxy is upstream and cyclical.
verdicts.bear: Score 13 reflects weak fundamentals/cash needs; +867% YTD signals extreme re-rating risk unrelated to this fundamental note.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Ramp of Tower InP epiwafer volumes for AI optical connectivity.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Thin balance sheet / cash burn at a sub-$1B cap; deal revenue slow to materialize.
